title: Baker Two Ways: A Catalan Coronation
title_candidates: Baker Two Ways: A Catalan Coronation
Stadium of Dreams, Tour of Tears
theme: Jon Baker turns a two-shot final-round deficit into a six-point Trophy win and pockets the Jacket too, while his brother Alex — leader on the eve of the last day — suffers the worst round of his TEG career at the very moment it mattered.
opening_hook: Open on Sunday at PGA Catalunya Stadium, hole 6: Alex Baker two clear, Jon Baker on his shoulder — a double bogey to draw level, and the tournament tilts. Then rewind to Thursday.
foreshadow: The Stadium Course's reputation as a championship test — and the fact TEG must come back to it on Sunday.
David Mullin's repeated cameos at the top of the Jacket leaderboard before slipping away.
Alex Baker's habit of finding one wreckage hole per round on the Tour course (b04) — a structural fragility waiting to be exposed.
John Patterson's bottomless capacity for the quintuple bogey — and the question of whether somebody else might out-stink him by Sunday.

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Jon led after R1 (35) and R2 (41), then a 32 at El Prat let Alex Baker overhaul him (level at R3 H1, outright at R3 H13) to take a 2-shot overnight lead. The decisive swing came on Sunday at the Stadium: Jon drew level at the 6th (b22) while Alex haemorrhaged points in three separate cold stretches (b40, b35, b36) en route to his worst-ever TEG round of 27 (b10). Jon's steady 35 turned a 2-down into a 6-clear win on 143 pts.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Jon led the gross every single round, but never comfortably: David Mullin kept drawing level (R3 H3, R4 H3) without ever going ahead outright. The decisive moment was R3 H6 at El Prat, where Jon went back ahead outright (b09) and held a 3-shot cushion across the last two rounds, closing at +70 to win by 3 — his third-best Jacket finish.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Patterson owned the basement after R1 (b33) and R2, kept down there by a steady drip of quintuples (b11, b12, b03). But Mullin's R3 collapsed at El Prat — a quadruple at the 6th (b17) and a bogey at the 12th (b21) dropped him to last, and a Sunday back-nine bleed (b41) sealed it. Final tally 125 — Spoon by being the only player to actually go backwards on the weekend.

headline_candidates: Jon Baker Strides Out at Spain's Showpiece
35 Plays 29: The Bakers Set the Tone
rounds.chosen_headline: Jon Baker Strides Out at Spain's Showpiece
rounds.angle: Jon posts 35 on the Stadium Course for a 3-point Trophy lead and a 2-shot Jacket lead; Patterson is already sinking, with three big numbers and a back-nine wreckage that drops him to the bottom of the Spoon race.

headline_candidates: Tour de Force, Tour de Farce
The Tour Course Bites Everyone Once
rounds.angle: Jon's 41 stretches the Jacket lead to 3 and keeps him 2 clear on the Trophy, but the round is defined by carnage at the 15th: Patterson's 11 (a sextuple) and Williams's 10 on the same hole, with Alex's 9 at the 12th completing the set.

headline_candidates: Alex Baker Steals the Lead at the New Course
rounds.angle: TEG's first visit to El Prat reshuffles everything: Alex draws level on the 1st (b37), goes outright ahead at the 13th (b14) after a clinical 12-14 stretch (b32), while Jon's 11-13 wobble (b43) gives it up. Meanwhile Jon hangs onto the Jacket outright at the 6th (b09), and Mullin's bogey at the 12th (b21) drops him to last in the Spoon race.

headline_candidates: Stadium Encore: Jon Closes, Alex Crumbles
rounds.angle: Back at the Stadium, Jon draws level at the 6th (b22) and never looks back; Alex bleeds 7 at 4-6 (b40), 8 at 12-14 (b35), 8 at 16-18 (b36) on the way to a career-worst 27 (b10). Jon's own 8 at the 13th (b15) is the only thing that keeps the margin under double figures. Mullin's late slide (b41) confirms the Spoon.

players.arc: Led wire-to-wire on the Jacket and from the front everywhere except a 24-hour blip at El Prat; absorbed Alex's surge, levelled at the Stadium 6th on Sunday, and pulled away to a 6-point Trophy / 3-shot Jacket double.

The story of the tournament until Sunday: outplayed his brother at El Prat to take an outright Trophy lead, then served up a career-worst 27 at the Stadium with three separate three-hole bleeds — 8 shots, 7 shots, 8 shots.

Jacket runner-up who kept drawing level on Jon without ever going past, and simultaneously the man who collected the Spoon — a back-nine slide on Sunday (b41) and that El Prat 6th (b17) the costliest moments.
Spent two days at the bottom of the Spoon race courtesy of an 11 and three 9s, escaped only because Mullin imploded harder at El Prat.
Made a habit of the quintuple bogey — one per round bar R3 — including a 10 at the Tour 15th and the 9 at the Stadium 10th that anchored an 11-shot Sunday stretch.

venue_notes: TEG's 2nd trip to Catalonia, anchored at Camiral/PGA Catalunya: the Stadium Course bookends the week (R1 and R4, its 2nd and 3rd TEG appearances), the gentler Tour course gets a single airing on Friday, and Saturday is a one-off detour to El Prat's Azul routing — a Greg Norman design, new to TEG, and the only course where Jon Baker did not lead the Trophy. Lean on the Stadium's championship pedigree to set up Sunday's coronation and the El Prat curio for the day everything wobbled.
